symbols: two figures embracing — the mystical union of the divine masculine and feminine aspects
purple and gold tones — royalty, divinity, and the splendor of God's presence
swirling patterns — the dynamic, ecstatic nature of divine love
description: A highly abstract, richly colored digital painting showing two intertwined figures in an intimate embrace. The forms are rendered in swirling purples, magentas, golds, and pinks, with ornamental patterns covering the bodies. The figures blend into each other and into a vibrant, flowing background. A gold ornate frame surrounds the image. Small blue angelic forms appear at the upper corners. The artist's watermark is visible at the lower left. The overall effect is lush, mystical, and ecstatic.
theological_description: The title references the Kabbalistic concept of Shekinah — God's indwelling presence, often personified as feminine — in mystical union with the divine. While this draws from Jewish mystical tradition rather than canonical Scripture, the concept of God's intimate presence with his people runs throughout the Bible. The Song of Solomon celebrates the passionate love between bride and bridegroom, which the Rabbis and Church Fathers read as an allegory of God's love for Israel/the Church (SNG.8.6-7). Hosea uses marital language for God's covenant faithfulness (HOS.2.19-20). Ezekiel describes the Shekinah glory departing and returning to the Temple (EZK.43.1-5). The New Testament culminates in Revelation 21:3: 'The dwelling place of God is with man. He will dwell with them, and they will be his people.'

scripture_refs.reason: Love strong as death — the passionate divine love represented in the embrace
God betrothing Israel to himself in faithfulness — the covenantal love depicted
The Shekinah glory returning to the Temple — the divine presence the title references
God dwelling with humanity — the eschatological fulfillment of the Shekinah concept

_meta.title: In the tenderness of the intimate embrace - Shekinah and Jehovah
